creator: markj@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to jSML4ThWwBID69YC from comment #2)
Hmm, that's a bit surprising.  If you look at "top -SHza" output while the benchmark is running, do you see an epair thread consuming 100% of a CPU?  There might be a bottleneck elsewhere.

(In reply to jSML4ThWwBID69YC from comment #3)
You'd also need to add "options PCBGROUP" to the kernel config.
